key_insight: High-velocity air fuel (HVAF) spraying can produce thick NiTi coatings that retain martensitic transformation and shape-memory functionality, even with some microstructural inhomogeneity.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical route to functional NiTi surface layers for wear-, corrosion-, and thermally responsive components where conventional coating methods struggle.
method: HVAF-sprayed NiTi onto mild steel using four processing parameter sets.
Characterized composition, porosity, microstructure, phase transformation, and thermomechanical response.
Compared as-sprayed and annealed coating behavior under bending, nanoindentation, and scratch loading.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports 100–300 µm coatings that adhered well to the substrate and showed martensitic transformation plus thermal actuation and shape-memory effects after annealing.
watch_item: As-sprayed coatings are microstructurally defective and require annealing; tensile strength remains limited, so processing control and service loading must be managed carefully.
paper_title: HVAF Spraying of NiTi Coatings: Microstructure, Phase Transformation and Shape Memory Behavior
